QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: Other Investment Products_6

question.text: The Public Provident Fund (PPF) has a lock-in period of:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** PPF has a 15-year tenure. Partial withdrawals are allowed from the 7th financial year, and loans are available between the 4th and 6th year. **The Trap:** 5 years is the premature closure period (allowed only for specific needs). Don't confuse premature closure eligibility with the full tenure. **Cross-Exam Context:** PPF features are tested in Series V-A (mutual fund comparison), X-A/X-B (financial planning), and XVII (retirement products). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** PPF offers EEE tax benefit (exempt at contribution under 80C, exempt on interest, exempt at maturity). This makes it one of the most tax-efficient retirement products.
